sections.content: Let me check the current position and see if I'm adjacent to the target now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current blue position and adjacency to targets
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
blue = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
charcoal = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'c'][0]
magenta = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'M'][0]
green = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N'][0]

print("Blue:", blue['boundary'][:4])
print("Charcoal:", charcoal['boundary'][:4])
print("Magenta:", magenta['boundary'][:4])
print("Green pixels:", green['pixels'])

# Check adjacency
adj = seg['adjacency_list']
blue_id = blue['id']
print("Blue adjacent to:", [p for p in adj if blue_id in p])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The blue block's right edge is at col 39, same as the targets. But the blue block's top is at row 12, while the targets end at row 7 (magenta bottom). I need to go up more to reach the targets.
I need to move UP to get the blue block closer. From row 12, I need to get to around row 8 or so to be adjacent to the magenta block at rows 4-7.

# Each UP moves 4 rows, so one UP would put blue at rows 8-11, still 1 row gap
# Two UPs would put blue at rows 4-7, overlapping with magenta
# Let me try one UP first

action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
